svg.text: run_simulation.py · input (6000 × 250 × 50) · one future per window · Student-t copula df=3 · itau / Kendall τ
keeps all workers saturated · bounds memory
not per task — avoids repeated pickling
all workers mmap data.npy read-only · OS page cache shared · _calc_fn_global set by initializer · NUMBA_NUM_THREADS=1
picks up next window_id from sliding window
zero idle time at tail — one future per window
future.result() UNPACKING (main process polling loop · time.sleep(0.005))
enqueue to ScalarWriter (every window) · enqueue to ObjectWriter (window_id % object_stride == 0)
SCALAR WRITER · every window · daemon thread
Queue (window_id, scalar_a=VaR, scalar_b=CVaR) for all 6000 windows
Condition sleeps until qsize ≥ flush_threshold (= n_workers × 4) or stop()
collect_scalars() → dict[window_id → (VaR, CVaR)] · checked vs _data_len (not n×chunk)
OBJECT WRITER · every object_stride windows · daemon thread
enqueue(): window_id % object_stride ≠ 0 → dropped before queue
Condition sleeps until qsize ≥ object_flush_threshold or stop()
6000 / object_stride objects saved · serialised via pickle.dump()
cleanup() removes all scalars_*.npy object_*.pkl input_data.npy
D · PER-WINDOW COPULA PIPELINE (runs inside each _worker call)
map to uniform via t CDF
window_id present in any scalar file → skip on restart
no off-by-one from partial last chunk
scalar queue depth before disk write = n_workers × 4
save copula samples every N windows (default 250)
pin numba per worker, avoid oversubscription
cleanup() removes all temp files after collection
run_simulation.py · ProcessPoolExecutor (spawn) · kendall_matrix_nb (numba njit, cache=True) · empirical_ppf (numba njit, cache=True)
ScalarWriter + ObjectWriter: daemon threads · threshold-based flush · Condition.wait_for() · zero CPU idle
one future per window → zero straggler idle time · all CPUs utilised until window 5999
